mandate: Create a dedicated partner login page at /partner-login with vendor-appropriate branding and auth flow. Update nav link and /partner layout redirect to point to /partner-login instead of /sign-in.
keyFindings: The /partner-login page and PartnerLoginClient component already existed from the partner-auth agent's work
The actual problem was routing: nav links pointed to /partner instead of /partner-login, causing auth gate bounce to /sign-in
The /partner layout had two redirect paths — line 13 already pointed to /partner-login but the catch block on line 21 still pointed to /sign-in
Both desktop and mobile nav links in the landing page needed updating
whatWorked: Reading existing code before creating anything. The page was already built — only the links were wrong. Joseph inheritance from partner-auth was accurate and prevented duplicate work.
whatFailed: Nothing failed. The mandate was simpler than expected because the page already existed.
recommendationsForNextAgent: When partner routing issues arise, check both the main redirect and the catch-block redirect in /partner/layout.tsx — they can diverge. The landing page has separate desktop and mobile nav sections that must be updated independently.
spiritContribution: Closed the last gap in the partner login experience. Vendors now have a clear, professional entry point instead of being confused by couple-oriented auth pages.
